summary:
Colistin belongs to the polymyxin group and is used clinically for resistant bacterial infections; it meets all criteria defined under standard medical classification systems as being classified as an antibiotic.

Answer: Yes
\boxed{Yes}

The summary focuses only on direct clinical relevance—colistin’s classification based on chemical structure (polymyxins), therapeutic purpose (treatment of drug-resistant gram-negative pathogens), and official recognition through authoritative references such as pharmaceutical compendia and treatment protocols—all aligning clearly with accepted definitions of antibiotics without deviation due to toxicity issues or usage limitations.

Colistin is recognized medically as part of the polymyxin antimicrobial class and specifically prescribed against serious multi-drug resistant Gram-negative bacterium infections—a core function defining antibiotics—and thus qualifies definitively as one according to current standards.
